Looking at addresses 0x401937-0x401953:
```
  401937:       0f 14 c2                unpcklps %xmm2,%xmm0
  40193a:       0f 14 c2                unpcklps %xmm2,%xmm0
  40193d:       83 c3 01                add    $0x1,%ebx
  401940:       0f 13 45 00             movlps %xmm0,0x0(%rbp)
  401944:       48 83 c5 0c             add    $0xc,%rbp

  40194d:       81 fb 20 03 00 00       cmp    $0x320,%ebx
  401953:       0f 84 9f 04 00 00       je     401df8 <main+0x6b8>
```

This is storing a pixel (3 floats) into the image buffer. Each pixel is 12 bytes (3 floats). The image buffer is an array of float* (pointers to pixel rows?).

Looking at allocate_image: it allocates an array of pointers, each pointing to a row of 800*3 floats = 2400 bytes = 0x960 bytes per row.
